#!/bin/bash

# Check if AIDE is installed
if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then
    # Install AIDE if it's not present
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Generate a new baseline integrity database
aide --init

# Install the generated baseline to its active location
aide --update

# Ensure the configuration file is in place and correctly configured
cat <<EOF > /etc/aide.conf
# AIDE configuration file for RHEL 8 systems

# General settings
log_file = "/var/log/aide.log"
database_file = "/var/lib/aide/aide.db"

# File inclusion patterns
include /etc
include /bin
include /sbin
include /lib
include /usr/bin
include /usr/sbin
include /usr/lib
include /var

# File exclusion patterns
exclude /dev
exclude /proc
exclude /sys
EOF

# Start the AIDE daemon in the background
systemctl start aide.service
systemctl enable aide.service

#!/bin/bash

# Check if AIDE is installed
if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then
    # Install AIDE if it's not present
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Generate a new baseline integrity database
aide --init

# Install the generated baseline to its active location
aide --update

# Ensure the configuration file is in place and correctly configured
cat <<EOF > /etc/aide.conf
# AIDE configuration file for RHEL 8 systems

# General settings
log_file = "/var/log/aide.log"
database_file = "/var/lib/aide/aide.db"

# File inclusion patterns
include /etc
include /bin
include /sbin
include /lib
include /usr/bin
include /usr/sbin
include /usr/lib
include /var

# File exclusion patterns
exclude /dev
exclude /proc
exclude /sys
EOF

# Start the AIDE daemon in the background
systemctl start aide.service
systemctl enable aide.service
```

#!/bin/bash

# Define audit tools to be monitored by AIDE
AUDIT_TOOLS="/usr/bin/auditctl /usr/bin/aureport /usr/bin/aucomm /usr/bin/autrace /usr/sbin/auditd"

# Create a directory for AIDE configuration if it does not exist
mkdir -p /etc/aide

# Define the AIDE configuration file path
AIDE_CONFIG="/etc/aide/aide.conf"

# Check if AIDE is installed and create an AIDE configuration file if not present
if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then
    echo "AIDE is not installed. Please install it before running this script."
    exit 1
else
    # Create the AIDE configuration file with the necessary rules for audit tools
    cat <<EOF > "$AIDE_CONFIG"
# AIDE configuration file for monitoring audit tools

# Include the default configuration file
include /etc/aide/default.aide.conf

# Define a new profile for audit tools
profile audit-tools {
    # Set the type of checks to perform (permissions, ownership, content)
    check = yes

    # Add rules for each audit tool
    $AUDIT_TOOLS {
        perm = yes
        own = yes
        md5 = yes
    }
}

# End of AIDE configuration file
EOF

    # Update the system's knowledge of the new AIDE configuration
    aide-update

    # Enable and start the AIDE service to monitor the audit tools
    systemctl enable --now aide.service
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Check if AIDE is already configured to send email notifications
if grep -qE '/usr/sbin/aide --check.*--email root' /etc/cron.daily/aide; then
    echo "AIDE email notification configuration already exists."
else
    # Create a script for daily AIDE check that sends email to root
    cat << 'EOF' > /etc/cron.daily/aide
#!/bin/bash

# Run AIDE integrity check and send results to root's email
/usr/sbin/aide --check --email root
EOF

    # Make the script executable
    chmod +x /etc/cron.daily/aide

    echo "AIDE email notification configuration added."
fi

# Ensure cron is running
systemctl start crond && systemctl enable crond

#!/bin/bash

# Check if AIDE configuration file exists
AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide/aide.conf"
if [ ! -f "$AIDE_CONF" ]; then
    echo "AIDE configuration file does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the original AIDE configuration file
cp "$AIDE_CONF" "$AIDE_CONF.bak"

# Modify the AIDE configuration to include ACL verification
sed -i '/^RuleSet/a \ RuleSet verify_acl' "$AIDE_CONF"

# Check if the modification was successful
if grep -q 'verify_acl' "$AIDE_CONF"; then
    echo "ACL verification has been added to all AIDE rulesets."
else
    echo "Failed to add ACL verification to AIDE rulesets. Restoring original configuration."
    cp "$AIDE_CONF.bak" "$AIDE_CONF"
    exit 1
fi

# Restart AIDE to apply the changes
systemctl restart aide

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Check if AIDE configuration file exists
CONFIG_FILE="/etc/aide/aide.conf"

if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    echo "AIDE configuration file does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

# Read all lines from the AIDE configuration file
while IFS= read -r line; do
    # Check if the line contains the keyword 'file' and is followed by a rule set
    if [[ $line == *'file' && $line != *'ignore' ]]; then
        # Append ',-a' to enable extended attribute checking for this file
        sed -i "s/$line/$line,-a/" "$CONFIG_FILE"
    fi
done < "$CONFIG_FILE"

# Restart AIDE to apply the changes
systemctl restart aide

#!/bin/bash

# Check if AIDE configuration file exists
CONFIG_FILE="/etc/aide/aide.conf"

if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    echo "AIDE configuration file does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

# Read all lines from the AIDE configuration file
while IFS= read -r line; do
    # Check if the line contains the keyword 'file' and is followed by a rule set
    if [[ $line == *'file' && $line != *'ignore' ]]; then
        # Append ',-a' to enable extended attribute checking for this file
        sed -i "s/$line/$line,-a/" "$CONFIG_FILE"
    fi
done < "$CONFIG_FILE"

# Restart AIDE to apply the changes
systemctl restart aide
```

#!/bin/bash

# Set FIPS mode to 2
setenforce 1

# Update system packages
dnf update -y

# Install necessary packages for FIPS compliance
dnf install -y nss-tools cryptsetup openssh-server openssh-clients

# Configure SSH to use FIPS compliant algorithms
sed -i '/^Ciphers/s/^/#/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
echo "Ciphers aes128-ctr,aes192-ctr,aes256-ctr,arcfour16,arcfour128" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Configure nss to use FIPS compliant algorithms
sed -i '/^nssecfg/s/^/#/' /etc/nss/nss.conf
echo "nssecfg = fips" >> /etc/nss/nss.conf

# Enable and start the sshd service
systemctl enable --now sshd

# Set SELinux to enforce mode on reboot
echo 'setenforce=1' > /etc/sysconfig/selinux
chmod 644 /etc/sysconfig/selinux

# Ensure the system remains in FIPS compliant mode after a reboot
echo "FIPS_MODE=ON" >> /etc/sysconfig/init

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Set FIPS mode to 2
setenforce 1

# Update system packages
dnf update -y

# Install necessary packages for FIPS compliance
dnf install -y nss-tools cryptsetup openssh-server openssh-clients

# Configure SSH to use FIPS compliant algorithms
sed -i '/^Ciphers/s/^/#/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
echo "Ciphers aes128-ctr,aes192-ctr,aes256-ctr,arcfour16,arcfour128" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Configure nss to use FIPS compliant algorithms
sed -i '/^nssecfg/s/^/#/' /etc/nss/nss.conf
echo "nssecfg = fips" >> /etc/nss/nss.conf

# Enable and start the sshd service
systemctl enable --now sshd

# Set SELinux to enforce mode on reboot
echo 'setenforce=1' > /etc/sysconfig/selinux
chmod 644 /etc/sysconfig/selinux

# Ensure the system remains in FIPS compliant mode after a reboot
echo "FIPS_MODE=ON" >> /etc/sysconfig/init
```

#!/bin/bash

# Set the default-strength cryptographic policy
cryptsetup-cli set-policy default-strength

# Ensure the policy is applied to all services by creating a symbolic link in /etc/crypto-policies
if [ ! -L /etc/crypto-policies/backends/system-backend ]; then
    ln -s /etc/crypto-policies/policy/default-strength /etc/crypto-policies/backends/system-backend
fi

# Update the system configuration to reflect the new policy
systemctl daemon-reload

# Set GnuTLS to defer to system-wide crypto policy

# Define FIPS 140-2 validated ciphers ordered strongest-to-weakest
fips_ciphers="chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com,aes256-gcm@openssh.com,aes128-gcm@openssh.com,aes256-cbc,aes192-cbc,aes128-cbc,arcfour-challenge,arcfour"

current_ciphers=$(grep -oP 'Ciphers\s*=\s*(.+)'" /etc/ssh/sshd_config)
if [[ "$current_ciphers" != "$fips_ciphers" ]]; then
    # Set the correct ciphers in sshd_config
    sed -i '/^Ciphers/s/^.*$/Ciphers '"$fips_ciphers"'/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config

    systemctl restart sshd
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Define FIPS 140-2 validated ciphers ordered strongest-to-weakest
fips_ciphers="chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com,aes256-gcm@openssh.com,aes128-gcm@openssh.com,aes256-cbc,aes192-cbc,aes128-cbc,arcfour-challenge,arcfour"

# Check if the SSH server is using the correct ciphers
current_ciphers=$(grep -oP 'Ciphers\s*=\s*(.+)'" /etc/ssh/sshd_config)
if [[ "$current_ciphers" != "$fips_ciphers" ]]; then
    # Set the correct ciphers in sshd_config
    sed -i '/^Ciphers/s/^.*$/Ciphers '"$fips_ciphers"'/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config

    # Restart SSH service to apply changes
    systemctl restart sshd
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure SSH server configuration is consistent with system crypto policy
SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
CRYPTO_POLICY="/etc/crypto-policies/backends/fips-algorithms.conf"

if grep -qE '^MACs[[:space:]]*[^a-z]' "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    sed -i '/^MACs[[:space:]]*\([^a-z]\)/d' "$SSHD_CONFIG"
fi

# Check if the system is in FIPS mode and set SSH server MAC algorithms accordingly
if grep -qE '^fips=1' "$CRYPTO_POLICY"; then
    echo "MACs hmac-sha2-256,hmac-sha2-512" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
else
    echo "MACs hmac-md5,hmac-ripemd160" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
fi

# Restart SSH service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

#!/bin/bash

# Create a directory for storing smartcard removal hooks if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /etc/gnome-session/gconf.d/

# Define the gsettings key to lock the screen on smartcard removal
key="org.gnome.desktop.session idle-lock"

# Create a GConf2 schema file if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f "/usr/share/glib-2.0/schemas/org.gnome.desktop.session.gschema.xml" ]; then
    gconftool-2 --create-schema /org/gnome/desktop/session
fi

# Set the gsettings key to lock the screen on smartcard removal
gconftool-2 --type bool --set $key true

# Create a GConf2 configuration file if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f "/etc/gnome-session/gconf.d/10-smartcard-lock.gconf" ]; then
    cat <<EOF > /etc/gnome-session/gconf.d/10-smartcard-lock.gconf
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<!DOCTYPE gconfschema PUBLIC "-//GNOME//DTD GConf Schema 2.0//EN" "http://www.gnome.orgschemas/gconf/2.0/gconfschema.dtd">
<gconfschema>
  <gconfkey file="/org/gnome/desktop/session">[...]</gconfkey>
</gconfschema>
EOF
fi

# Create a script to lock the screen when smartcard is removed
cat <<'EOF' > /etc/polkit-1/localauthority/50-local.d/10-smartcard-lock.pkla
[PolicyKit1]
Identity=unix-group:smartcard-users
Action=org.gnome.SessionManager idle-lock
ResultCode=allow
EOF

# Create a script to be executed when smartcard is removed
cat <<'EOF' > /etc/polkit-1/localauthority/50-local.d/10-smartcard-unlock.pkla
[PolicyKit1]
Identity=unix-group:smartcard-users
Action=org.gnome.SessionManager unlock-screen
ResultCode=allow
EOF

# Create a systemd service to handle smartcard removal
cat <<'EOF' > /etc/systemd/system/smartcard-removal.service
[Unit]
Description=Smartcard Removal Service

[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/bin/gnome-screensaver-command -l
Type=oneshot

[Install]
WantedBy=sessions.target
EOF

# Enable the systemd service to start on smartcard removal
systemctl enable smartcard-removal.service

# Create a udev rule to trigger the systemd service on smartcard removal
cat <<'EOF' > /etc/udev/rules.d/90-smartcard-removal.rules
ACTION=="remove", SUBSYSTEM=="usb", GOTO="smartcard_remove"
ACTION=="add", SUBSYSTEM=="usb", GOTO="smartcard_add"

:smartcard_remove
RUN+="/usr/bin/systemctl start smartcard-removal.service"

:smartcard_add
EOF

# Apply the udev rule
udevadm control --reload-rules && udevadm trigger

# Add the group 'smartcard-users' if it doesn't exist
if ! getent group | grep -q '^smartcard-users$'; then
    groupadd smartcard-users
fi

# Add the necessary permissions for the group
chmod 660 /etc/gnome-session/gconf.d/10-smartcard-lock.gconf
chown root:root /etc/gnome-session/gconf.d/10-smartcard-lock.gconf
chmod 755 /etc/systemd/system/smartcard-removal.service
chmod 644 /etc/polkit-1/localauthority/50-local.d/10-smartcard-lock.pkla
chmod 644 /etc/polkit-1/localauthority/50-local.d/10-smartcard-unlock.pkla

# Add users to the 'smartcard-users' group
for user in $(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1); do
    gpasswd -a $user smartcard-users
done

    echo "sudoers file does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

# Use visudo to edit the sudoers file
visudo <<EOF
# Set default timeout for sudoers
 Defaults timeout=$DEFAULT_TIMEOUT

# Ensure that the default timeout is applied to all users
%wheel   ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL
EOF

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Check if the necessary tooling is already installed
if ! rpm -q rng-tools &> /dev/null; then
    # If not, install it
    yum install -y rng-tools
fi

# Configure the hardware random number generator to be used by the kernel
echo "HRNG0" > /etc/sysconfig/rng-tools

# Ensure the entropy gathering service is enabled and started
systemctl enable rngd
systemctl start rngd
```

#!/bin/bash

# Remove Red Hat Support reporting plugin if present
if rpm -q --queryformat='%{NAME}' redhat-support-plugin > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum remove -y redhat-support-plugin
fi

# Ensure the plugin is not installed in future updates

[no-redhat-support]
name=No Red Hat Support Plugin
baseurl=http://localhost/no-redhat-support
enabled=0
gpgcheck=0
EOF

# Remove any existing plugin files if still present
find / -type f -name 'redhat-support-plugin*' -exec rm -f {} \; 2>/dev/null

#!/bin/bash

# Remove Red Hat Support reporting plugin if present
if rpm -q --queryformat='%{NAME}' redhat-support-plugin > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum remove -y redhat-support-plugin
fi

# Ensure the plugin is not installed in future updates
cat <<EOF | tee /etc/yum.repos.d/dont-install-redhat-support.repo
[no-redhat-support]
name=No Red Hat Support Plugin
baseurl=http://localhost/no-redhat-support
enabled=0
gpgcheck=0
EOF

# Remove any existing plugin files if still present
find / -type f -name 'redhat-support-plugin*' -exec rm -f {} \; 2>/dev/null
```

#!/bin/bash

# Set up GPG for signature verification
if ! grep -qE 'gpg: /etc/apt/trusted.gpg' /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/00apt-gpg; then
    echo "Acquire::GPGCheck \"true\";" | sudo tee -a /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/00apt-gpg
fi

# Ensure the package manager uses gpg for signature verification
if ! grep -qE 'APT::Get::Insecure::Allow-Unauthenticated "false";' /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/; then

fi

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Check if profile-based authentication tool is available
if ! command -v authconfig &> /dev/null; then
    echo "authconfig is not installed."
    exit 1
fi

# Select and activate a supported authentication profile
authconfig --updateall --enablemd5 --enableshadow --useshadowpasswords=yes --enablepamkrb5 --enableldap --enableldapauth --enablemsad --enablensswitchldap --enablenscd --enablecaching --setauthconfigfile=/etc/authconfig/authconfig.conf

# Check if the configuration was successfully updated
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "User authentication is configured through profile-based tool."
else
    echo "Failed to configure user authentication through profile-based tool."
    exit 1
fi
```

#!/bin/bash

# Define the required login banner content
banner_content="This system is for authorized use only. Use of this system is subject to all applicable local, state and federal laws. All activities of users on this system may be monitored and recorded. No expectation of privacy should be assumed. By logging in, you consent to these conditions."

# Check if the banner file exists
if [ ! -f /etc/issue ]; then
    # Create the banner file if it does not exist
    touch /etc/issue
fi

# Set the login banner content
echo "$banner_content" > /etc/issue

# Ensure that the console logins will display this banner
echo 'Banner /etc/issue' >> /etc/login.defs

#!/bin/bash

# Define the required login banner content
banner_content="This system is for authorized use only. Use of this system is subject to all applicable local, state and federal laws. All activities of users on this system may be monitored and recorded. No expectation of privacy should be assumed. By logging in, you consent to these conditions."

# Check if the banner file exists
if [ ! -f /etc/issue ]; then
    # Create the banner file if it does not exist
    touch /etc/issue
fi

# Set the login banner content
echo "$banner_content" > /etc/issue

# Ensure that the console logins will display this banner
echo 'Banner /etc/issue' >> /etc/login.defs
```

[DAEMONS]

port = 22
filter = sshd
logpath = /var/log/auth.log

findtime = 600

fi

MAX_ATTEMPTS=3

# Check if the pam_tally2 module is already configured in the authentication subsystem
if grep -qE 'pam_tally2.so' /etc/pam.d/common-auth; then
    # If it is, ensure that the lockout policy is set correctly
    sed -i '/pam_tally2.so/ s/^session.*//g' /etc/pam.d/common-auth
    sed -i '/pam_tally2.so/ s/^account.*//g' /etc/pam.d/common-auth
    echo "session required pam_tally2.so onerr=fail audit silent even_deny_root reset=$MAX_ATTEMPTS" >> /etc/pam.d/common-auth
else

    echo "auth required pam_tally2.so onerr=fail audit silent even_deny_root" >> /etc/pam.d/common-auth
    echo "account required pam_tally2.so onerr=fail audit silent even_deny_root reset=$MAX_ATTEMPTS" >> /etc/pam.d/common-auth
fi

# Ensure the changes persist across reboots by making sure the pam_tally2 module is also configured in other relevant files
for file in /etc/pam.d/*.conf; do
    if grep -qE 'pam_tally2.so' "$file"; then
        sed -i '/pam_tally2.so/ s/^session.*//g' "$file"

        echo "session required pam_tally2.so onerr=fail audit silent even_deny_root reset=$MAX_ATTEMPTS" >> "$file"

        echo "auth required pam_tally2.so onerr=fail audit silent even_deny_root" >> "$file"
        echo "account required pam_tally2.so onerr=fail audit silent even_deny_root reset=$MAX_ATTEMPTS" >> "$file"
    fi
done

# Restart the authentication service to apply the changes
systemctl restart authselect | true

umask 077

if grep -qE 'pam_tally2.so.*silent' /etc/pam.d/*; then
    # If already configured, exit to avoid unnecessary changes
    exit 0
fi

    # Check if the file is not a directory and contains the word 'auth'
    if [ ! -d "$pam_file" ] && grep -qE '^auth\s'; then
        # Use sed to add the silent option to the pam_tally2 module
        sed -i '/pam_tally2.so/ s/^.*$/& silent/' "$pam_file"
    fi
done

# Set the PAM configuration files to be owned by root and have strict permissions
chown root:root /etc/pam.d/*
chmod 644 /etc/pam.d/*

# Ensure the changes persist across reboots by setting the right permissions on the pam configuration directory
chown root:root /etc/pam.d

check_password_complexity() {

    local newpass="$2"

    # Calculate the number of differing characters
    local diff_chars=$(echo -n "$oldpass" | grep -o . | sort | uniq | wc -l)
    local common_chars=$(tr '[A-Za-z0-9]' '\n' < /dev/urandom | head -c 8 | tr -d '\n')

    fi

    fi
done

# Define the maximum number of consecutive characters from the same class
MAX_CONSECUTIVE=4

# Function to check password quality
check_password_quality() {
    local password="$1"
    local upper_count=$(echo "$password" | grep -o '[A-Z]' | wc -l)
    local lower_count=$(echo "$password" | grep -o '[a-z]' | wc -l)
    local digit_count=$(echo "$password" | grep -o '[0-9]' | wc -l)
    local symbol_count=$(echo "$password" | grep -o '[^A-Za-z0-9]' | wc -l)

    # Check for consecutive characters from the same class

    elif [[ $(echo "$password" | grep -o '[a-z]\{5,\}') != "" ]]; then

    elif [[ $(echo "$password" | grep -o '[0-9]\{5,\}') != "" ]]; then
        return 1
    elif [[ $(echo "$password" | grep -o '[^A-Za-z0-9]\{5,\}') != "" ]]; then
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if the password meets the minimum length requirement
    local total_length=$((upper_count + lower_count + digit_count + symbol_count))
    if [ $total_length -lt 8 ]; then

    fi

    while [[ ${#password} -ge 4 ]]; do
        local current_char="${password:0:1}"
        local next_char="${password:1:1}"
        if [[ "${current_char}" == "${next_char}" ]]; then
            local count=2
            while [[ "${password:${count}:1}" == "${current_char}" && ${#password} -ge (${count}+1) ]]; do

            fi

}

        echo "Password rejected. Please avoid using more than three consecutive identical characters."
    else
        echo "$newpass" | passwd --stdin root

    fi
done

    if [[ $(tr -d '[:space:]' <<< "$password") =~ $upper ]] && \

#!/bin/bash

# Set maximum password age to 365 days
MAX_PASS_AGE=365

# Get all local users except root
USERS=$(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1 | grep -v '^root$')

for USER in $USERS; do
    # Check current password age for the user
    PASS_AGE=$(chage -l "$USER" | grep 'Password expires' | awk '{print $4}' | tr -d ' ')

    if [ -z "$PASS_AGE" ]; then
        # User does not have a password age set, set it to MAX_PASS_AGE
        chage -M $MAX_PASS_AGE "$USER"
    elif [ "$PASS_AGE" -ne "$MAX_PASS_AGE" ]; then
        # User has a different password age, update it to MAX_PASS_AGE
        chage -M $MAX_PASS_AGE "$USER"
    fi
done

#!/bin/bash

# Get all users who have changed their password within the last 24 hours
users=$(chage -l root | grep 'Password was set' | awk '{print $1}')

# Loop through each user and enforce a minimum password age of 1 day
for user in $users; do
    # Get the current password age for the user
    pass_age=$(chage -l "$user" | grep 'Password was set' | awk '{print $4}' | cut -d'.' -f2)

    # Calculate the date when the password should have been changed
    change_date=$(date --date="$pass_age days ago" +%s)

    # Get the last login time for the user
    last_login=$(last "$user" | grep -v '^$' | awk '{print $5}' | head -n 1)

    # If the password was set more than 24 hours ago, it's non-compliant
    if [[ $(date --date="$change_date + 1 day" +%s) -le $(date -d "$last_login" +%s) ]]; then
        # Set a new password for the user
        echo "root:$(openssl rand -base64 12)" | chpasswd

        # Update the last login time to ensure it's within the minimum age requirement
        passwd -e "$user"
    fi
done

users=$(chage -l root | grep 'Password was set' | awk '{print $1}')

# Loop through each user and enforce a minimum password age of 1 day
for user in $users; do
    # Get the current password age for the user
    pass_age=$(chage -l "$user" | grep 'Password was set' | awk '{print $4}' | cut -d'.' -f2)

    # Calculate the date when the password should have been changed
    change_date=$(date --date="$pass_age days ago" +%s)

    # Get the last login time for the user
    last_login=$(last "$user" | grep -v '^$' | awk '{print $5}' | head -n 1)

    # If the password was set more than 24 hours ago, it's non-compliant
    if [[ $(date --date="$change_date + 1 day" +%s) -le $(date -d "$last_login" +%s) ]]; then

#!/bin/bash

# Get all local interactive users
users=$(getent passwd | grep '/home/' | cut -d: -f1)

for user in $users; do
    # Get the primary group of the user
    primary_group=$(id -gn "$user")

    # Set the umask to ensure new files are group-owned by the user's primary group
    umask 0007

    # Loop through all files and directories in the user's home directory
    for entry in $(find /home/"$user" -type f -o -type d); do
        # Get the current group ownership of the file or directory
        current_group=$(ls -ld "$entry" | awk '{print $4}')

        # If the current group is not the user's primary group, change it
        if [ "$current_group" != "$primary_group" ]; then
            chgrp "$primary_group" "$entry"
        fi
    done
done

# Define the minimum permissions for dot-files
MIN_PERMS=0740

# Function to check and set file permissions
check_and_set_perms() {
    local file="$1"
    local perms=$(stat -c "%a" "$file")
    if [ "$perms" -gt "$MIN_PERMS" ]; then
        chmod 740 "$file"
    fi
}

# Get a list of all users including root
USERS=$(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1)

# Loop through each user and check their dot-files
for user in $USERS; do
    # Check if the user has a home directory
    HOMEDIR=$(eval echo ~$user)
    if [ -d "$HOMEDIR" ]; then
        # Define the dot-file paths
        DOT_FILES=(.bashrc .profile .bash_profile .bash_login .login .env .bash_logout)

        # Loop through each dot-file and set permissions
        for file in "${DOT_FILES[@]}"; do
            FILE_PATH="$HOMEDIR/$file"
            if [ -f "$FILE_PATH" ]; then
                check_and_set_perms "$FILE_PATH"
            fi
        done
    fi
done

# Check and set permissions for root's dot-files
check_and_set_perms "/root/.bashrc"

check_and_set_perms "/root/.bash_login"

#!/bin/bash

BOOT_PARTITION="/boot"
MOUNT_OPTIONS="nosuid,nogroup"

# Check if the boot partition is mounted
if mountpoint -q "$BOOT_PARTITION"; then
    # Get current mount options for the boot partition
    CURRENT_OPTIONS=$(mount | grep "$BOOT_PARTITION" | awk '{print $5}' | cut -d, -f2-)

    # Check if the required options are already set
    if [[ "$CURRENT_OPTIONS" != *"$MOUNT_OPTIONS"* ]]; then
        # Set the mount options to prevent honoring SUID/SGID bits
        echo "Setting mount options for $BOOT_PARTITION"
        umount -l "$BOOT_PARTITION"
        mount -o remount,"$MOUNT_OPTIONS" "$BOOT_PARTITION"
    else
        echo "Mount options are already set correctly for $BOOT_PARTITION"
    fi
else
    # If the boot partition is not mounted, there's nothing to do
    echo "Boot partition $BOOT_PARTITION is not mounted"
fi

# Update /etc/fstab to persist the changes across reboots
if grep -qE "^$BOOT_PARTITION" /etc/fstab; then
    # Get current options from fstab
    CURRENT_OPTIONS=$(grep -E "^$BOOT_PARTITION" /etc/fstab | awk '{print $4}')

    # Check if the required options are already set in fstab
    if [[ "$CURRENT_OPTIONS" != *"$MOUNT_OPTIONS"* ]]; then
        # Update fstab to include the mount options

    else
        echo "Mount options are already set correctly in /etc/fstab for $BOOT_PARTITION"
    fi
else
    # If the boot partition is not listed in fstab, there's nothing to do
    echo "Boot partition $BOOT_PARTITION is not listed in /etc/fstab"
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Check if SELinux is enabled
if ! grep -qE 'SELINUX=.*-enforcing' /etc/selinux/config; then
    echo "SELinux is not configured to enforce mode."
    exit 1
fi

# Install the file access policy daemon software (apparmor)
if ! rpm -q apparmor > /dev/null; then
    yum install -y apparmor
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "Failed to install AppArmor."
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Enable and start the AppArmor service
systemctl enable --now apparmor.service
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Failed to enable or start AppArmor service."
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure AppArmor profiles are loaded
for profile in /etc/apparmor.d/*; do
    aa-enforce $profile
done

echo "Application whitelisting capability is now available."
```

#!/bin/bash

# Install AppArmor if not already installed
if ! rpm -q apparmor > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    sudo dnf install -y apparmor
fi

# Load AppArmor profiles
sudo systemctl start apparmor

# Set the default profile to enforcing
sudo aa-complain -D

# Create a new profile with deny-all rule
profile_name="app_allowlist"
sudo aa-logprof -c /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name -p /usr/sbin/aa-logprof -d -i

# Add deny-all rule to the profile
echo "denied / /home/* r," >> /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name
echo "denied / /var/log/* r," >> /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name
echo "denied / /etc/* r," >> /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name
echo "denied / /usr/local/* r," >> /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name

# Add the permit-by-exception rules for allowed binaries
allowed_binaries="/bin/bash /usr/bin/python3 /usr/sbin/named /usr/sbin/sshd"
for binary in $allowed_binaries; do
    echo "allow /$binary r," >> /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name
done

# Enable the new profile
sudo aa-enforce -f /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name

# Set the system to use the new profile on boot
sudo systemctl enable apparmor.service

#!/bin/bash

# Install AppArmor if not already installed
if ! rpm -q apparmor > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    sudo dnf install -y apparmor
fi

# Load AppArmor profiles
sudo systemctl start apparmor

# Set the default profile to enforcing
sudo aa-complain -D

# Create a new profile with deny-all rule
profile_name="app_allowlist"
sudo aa-logprof -c /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name -p /usr/sbin/aa-logprof -d -i

# Add deny-all rule to the profile
echo "denied / /home/* r," >> /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name
echo "denied / /var/log/* r," >> /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name
echo "denied / /etc/* r," >> /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name
echo "denied / /usr/local/* r," >> /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name

# Add the permit-by-exception rules for allowed binaries
allowed_binaries="/bin/bash /usr/bin/python3 /usr/sbin/named /usr/sbin/sshd"
for binary in $allowed_binaries; do
    echo "allow /$binary r," >> /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name
done

# Enable the new profile
sudo aa-enforce -f /etc/apparmor.d/$profile_name

# Set the system to use the new profile on boot
sudo systemctl enable apparmor.service

    echo "Maximum polling interval not defined for all servers, please check ntp.conf."
else
    echo "Maximum polling interval set to $MAX_INTERVAL seconds for all servers."

#!/bin/bash

# Define the list of SSH-related files and directories to check
SSH_FILES=(~/.ssh/authorized_keys ~/.ssh/config)

# Function to check file permissions
check_permissions() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -w "$file" ] && ! [ -r "$file" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: $file is world-writable."
        return 1
    fi
    if [ -d "$file" ] && [ -w "$(dirname "$file")" ] && ! [ -x "$file" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Directory $(dirname $file) is world-writable."
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

# Check each SSH-related file for correct permissions
for file in "${SSH_FILES[@]}"; do
    if [ -e "$file" ]; then
        check_permissions "$file"
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            exit 1
        fi
    else
        echo "ERROR: $file does not exist."
        exit 1
    fi
done

# Check the ownership of the user's home directory and SSH-related files
for file in "${SSH_FILES[@]}"; do
    if [ -e "$file" ]; then
        owner=$(stat -c '%U' "$file")
        if [ "$owner" != "root" ] && [ "$owner" != "$(whoami)" ]; then
            echo "ERROR: $file is not owned by root or the current user."
            exit 1
        fi
    fi
done

# If all checks pass, ensure SSH configuration files are set to be enforced on reboot
chmod 700 /home/$(whoami)/.ssh
chown root:root /home/$(whoami)/.ssh
for file in "${SSH_FILES[@]}"; do
    if [ -e "$file" ]; then
        chmod 600 "$file"
        chown $(whoami):$(whoami) "$file"
    fi
done

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Check if SSH configuration file exists
if [ ! -f /etc/ssh/sshd_config ]; then
    echo "SSH configuration file does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the original sshd_config
cp /etc/ssh/sshd_config /etc/ssh/sshd_config.bak

# Read current SSH configuration and modify it if necessary
while IFS= read -r line; do
    case "$line" in
        *ClientAliveInterval=*|*ClientAliveCountMax=*|*ServerAliveInterval=*|*ServerAliveCountMax=*|*KeyRegenerationInterval=*|*RekeyLimit=*|*UsePrivilegeSeparation=*|*PermitUserEnvironment=*)
            # Skip existing settings
            ;;
        *)
            echo "$line" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.new
            ;;
    esac
done < /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Add new configuration options
echo "ClientAliveInterval 60" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.new
echo "ClientAliveCountMax 3" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.new
echo "ServerAliveInterval 60" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.new
echo "ServerAliveCountMax 3" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.new
echo "KeyRegenerationInterval 3600" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.new
echo "RekeyLimit 512M" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.new
echo "UsePrivilegeSeparation yes" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.new
echo "PermitUserEnvironment no" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.new

# Replace the original sshd_config with the modified one
mv /etc/ssh/sshd_config.new /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Restart SSH service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

fi

#!/bin/bash

# Check if udev is installed
if ! rpm -q udev > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Install udev
    dnf install -y udev
fi

# Enable and start udev rules service
systemctl enable --now udev-rules.service

# Create or update the USB device authorization file if it does not exist or is outdated
if [ ! -f /etc/udev/rules.d/70-usb-permissions.rules ]; then
    cat <<EOF > /etc/udev/rules.d/70-usb-permissions.rules
SUBSYSTEM=="usb", ACTION=="add", RUN+="/usr/libexec/usbguard/usbguard-control --allow"
SUBSYSTEM=="usb", ACTION=="remove", RUN+="/usr/libexec/usbguard/usbguard-control --block"
EOF
else
    # Update the existing rules file
    sed -i '/SUBSYSTEM=="usb"/a\RUN+="/usr/libexec/usbguard/usbguard-control --allow"' /etc/udev/rules.d/70-usb-permissions.rules
    sed -i '/SUBSYSTEM=="usb"/a\RUN+="/usr/libexec/usbguard/usbguard-control --block"' /etc/udev/rules.d/70-usb-permissions.rules
fi

# Ensure usbguard service is installed and enabled

fi

#!/bin/bash

# Create USBGuard configuration directory if it does not exist
mkdir -p /etc/usbguard

# Get currently connected USB devices
connected_devices=$(lsusb)

# Generate a new policy file with permissions for all connected devices
echo "$connected_devices" | while read device; do
    # Extract the bus and device numbers from the device string
    bus=$(echo $device | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's/://')
    device_id=$(echo $device | awk '{print $3}' | sed 's/://')

    # Create a policy entry for each connected device
    echo "allow = [bus=$bus, device=$device_id]" >> /etc/usbguard/initial_policy.conf

done

# Enable USBGuard service to apply the new policy and ensure it starts on boot
systemctl enable usbguard.service
systemctl start usbguard.service

# Generate a new policy file with permissions for all connected devices
echo "$connected_devices" | while read device; do
    # Extract the bus and device numbers from the device string
    bus=$(echo $device | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's/://')

echo "audit_queue_length = 8192" | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.conf >/dev/null

# Apply the sysctl settings immediately
sudo sysctl -p

# Create or update the audit configuration file with the new queue length
sudo sed -i '/^audit_queue_length/ s/.*/audit_queue_length = 8192/' /etc/audit/auditd.conf

# Ensure the audit daemon is restarted to apply the changes
sudo systemctl restart auditd

# Lock audit configuration by setting immutable flag on the audit rules file
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
if [ ! -e "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE.immutable" ]; then
    touch "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE.immutable"
fi

# Ensure that the immutable flag is set in the audit configuration
cat <<EOF > /etc/audit/auditd.conf
# Set this to yes to make the audit rules immutable at runtime.
immutable_audit_rules=yes
EOF

# Create a systemd drop-in file to ensure the immutable flag persists after reboot
cat <<EOF > /etc/systemd/system/auditd.service.d/immutable.conf
[Service]
ExecStart=
ExecReload=/bin/true
Restart=on-failure
EOF

# Ensure that the audit daemon is restarted to apply changes
systemctl restart auditd

    auditctl -a -F arch=b32,b64,permitted=0 -S chmod -k $KEY
    auditctl -a -F arch=b32,b64,permitted=0 -S chown -k $KEY

if command -v systemctl &> /dev/null; then
    # Enable and start the auditd service if it is not already running
    systemctl enable --now auditd
else
    # For systems without systemctl, ensure auditd is started and enabled at boot

    service auditd start

if [ -f /etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod_by_user.rules ]; then
    echo '/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod_by_user.rules' >> /etc/audit/audit.rules

    auditctl -a -F arch=b32,b64,permitted=0 -S chown -k $KEY
    auditctl -a -F arch=b32,b64,permitted=0 -S chgrp -k $KEY
else
    # Load the rule using augenrules if auditctl is not available
    echo 'type=AVCMSG msg="User with UID >= 1000 attempted to modify file permissions" key=$KEY' > /etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod_by_user.rules
    auditctl -r /etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod_by_user.rules
fi

# Make sure the rules are persistent across reboots
if command -v systemctl &> /dev/null; then
    # Enable and start the auditd service if it is not already running
    systemctl enable --now auditd
else
    # For systems without systemctl, ensure auditd is started and enabled at boot
    chkconfig auditd on
    service auditd start
fi

# Ensure that the audit rules are loaded when the system boots
if [ -f /etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod_by_user.rules ]; then
    echo '/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod_by_user.rules' >> /etc/audit/audit.rules
fi
```

    echo '-a always,exit -F arch=b32,b64 -S truncate -F key=access -F uid>=1000 -F auid>=1000' >> /etc/audit/audit.rules
fi

# Update audit configuration to include permission-denied and operation-not-permitted errors
if ! grep -qE '^-a always,exit -F arch=b32,b64 -S truncate -F key=access -F exit=-EPERM,-EACCES' /etc/audit/audit.rules; then
    echo '-a always,exit -F arch=b32,b64 -S truncate -F key=access -F exit=-EPERM,-EACCES' >> /etc/audit/audit.rules
fi

# Reload audit daemon configuration
systemctl restart auditd
```

#!/bin/bash

# Load audit configuration
CONFIG_FILE="/etc/audit/auditd.conf"

# Check if the configuration file exists
if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Audit configuration file does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

# Read current action on disk error
ACTION=$(grep '^action_on_disk_error' "$CONFIG_FILE" | cut -d '=' -f2)

# If the action is not set to single, change it
if [ "$ACTION" != "single" ]; then
    # Backup original configuration file
    cp "$CONFIG_FILE" "$CONFIG_FILE.bak"

    # Set the action on disk error to single
    sed -i 's/^action_on_disk_error=\(.*\)$/action_on_disk_error=single/' "$CONFIG_FILE"
    
    # Verify the change was made
    ACTION=$(grep '^action_on_disk_error' "$CONFIG_FILE" | cut -d '=' -f2)
    if [ "$ACTION" == "single" ]; then
        echo "Configuration updated to switch to single-user mode on disk error."
    else
        echo "Failed to update configuration. Restoring original file."
        mv "$CONFIG_FILE.bak" "$CONFIG_FILE"
        exit 1

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Load audit configuration
CONFIG_FILE="/etc/audit/auditd.conf"

# Check if the configuration file exists
if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Audit configuration file does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

# Read current action on disk error
ACTION=$(grep '^action_on_disk_error' "$CONFIG_FILE" | cut -d '=' -f2)

# If the action is not set to single, change it
if [ "$ACTION" != "single" ]; then
    # Backup original configuration file
    cp "$CONFIG_FILE" "$CONFIG_FILE.bak"

    # Set the action on disk error to single
    sed -i 's/^action_on_disk_error=\(.*\)$/action_on_disk_error=single/' "$CONFIG_FILE"
    
    # Verify the change was made
    ACTION=$(grep '^action_on_disk_error' "$CONFIG_FILE" | cut -d '=' -f2)
    if [ "$ACTION" == "single" ]; then
        echo "Configuration updated to switch to single-user mode on disk error."
    else
        echo "Failed to update configuration. Restoring original file."
        mv "$CONFIG_FILE.bak" "$CONFIG_FILE"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    echo "Configuration is already set to switch to single-user mode on disk error."
fi

exit 0
```

#!/bin/bash

# Set low disk space threshold to 10%
LOW_SPACE_THRESHOLD=10

# Configure auditd to send email alerts when disk space is low
cat <<EOF > /etc/audit/auditd.conf.d/low-disk-space.conf
log_storage_full = yes
email_alert = yes
alert_email = admin@example.com
low_space_threshold = $LOW_SPACE_THRESHOLD
EOF

# Ensure the configuration file is loaded
systemctl restart auditd

# Set the script to be executed on low disk space
cat <<EOF > /etc/cron.d/audit-low-disk-space
*/5 * * * * root /usr/local/bin/audit_low_disk_space.sh >> /var/log/audit_low_disk_space.log 2>&1
EOF

# Create a script to check disk space and send email if below threshold
cat <<EOF > /usr/local/bin/audit_low_disk_space.sh
#!/bin/bash

# Check available disk space on the root partition
AVAILABLE_SPACE=$(df --output=avail / | tail -n 1)

# Calculate the low space threshold in percentage
THRESHOLD_PERCENT=$((LOW_SPACE_THRESHOLD * 1024 / 1048576))

# Compare available space with the threshold and send email if below threshold
if [ "$AVAILABLE_SPACE" -lt "$((THRESHOLD_PERCENT))" ]; then
    echo "Low disk space alert: Available space is less than $LOW_SPACE_THRESHOLD%" | mail -s "Audit Low Disk Space Alert" admin@example.com
fi
EOF

# Make the script executable
chmod +x /usr/local/bin/audit_low_disk_space.sh

# Ensure the cron job has the correct permissions to execute the script
chown root:root /etc/cron.d/audit-low-disk-space
chmod 644 /etc/cron.d/audit-low-disk-space

#!/bin/bash

# Define the path to the audit log directory and the threshold percentage
AUDIT_LOG_DIR="/var/log/audit"
THRESHOLD=25

# Calculate the disk usage in percentage for the audit log directory
usage=$(df -h "$AUDIT_LOG_DIR" | tail -n 1 | awk '{print $5}' | sed 's/%//')

# Check if the free space is below the threshold
if [ "$usage" -le "$THRESHOLD" ]; then
    # Set the low-space notification action to write a warning message to /var/log/messages
    auditctl -w "$AUDIT_LOG_DIR" -p war -k LOW_SPACE
else
    # If above the threshold, remove the low-space alert if it exists
    auditctl -l | grep LOW_SPACE && auditctl -d -k LOW_SPACE
fi

# Ensure the audit daemon is configured to notify on low space
cat <<EOF > /etc/audit/auditd.conf
low_space_action = 1
low_space_percentage = $THRESHOLD
EOF

# Restart the audit daemon to apply changes
systemctl restart auditd
